name: secret_rotation
description: a secrets/credential rotation world with leak risk; actions create, rotate, revoke

card.description: a secrets/credential rotation world with leak risk; actions create, rotate, revoke

rules: Time advances one tick per action; every active credential ages by one tick each step.
An active credential whose age reaches the leak threshold (5) is compromised: it is marked leaked and the lifetime 'leaks' counter ticks.
'create' adds a fresh active credential at age 0 (params.id names it, else it is auto-named); creating a duplicate id is a no-op.
'rotate' resets a credential's age to 0, bumps its version and clears its leak; with no params.id it rotates the oldest active credential.

'revoke' retires a credential (status 'revoked'), removing it from the risk surface; with no params.id it retires the most dangerous active one (a compromised credential first, else the oldest) --- this is how a leak is contained.

Aggregates always describe the active credentials only: 'active' counts them, 'exposure' sums their ages, 'at_risk' counts those aged >= 3 and not yet leaked, 'compromised' counts those currently leaked.

perception.code:
def perceive(data):
    LEAK, WARN = 5, 3
    secrets = []
    for line in str(data).splitlines():
        line = line.strip()
        if not line:
            continue
        toks = line.replace(',', ' ').split()
        sid = toks[0]
        age = 0
        for t in toks[1:]:
            tt = t.split('=')[-1]
            if tt.lstrip('-').isdigit():
                age = max(0, int(tt))
                break
        status = "revoked" if any(t.lower() == "revoked" for t in toks[1:]) else "active"
        secrets.append({"id": sid, "version": 1, "status": status,
                        "age": age, "leaked": status == "active" and age >= LEAK})
    active = [x for x in secrets if x["status"] == "active"]
    return {
        "secrets": secrets,
        "active": len(active),
        "at_risk": sum(1 for x in active if x["age"] >= WARN and not x["leaked"]),
        "compromised": sum(1 for x in active if x["leaked"]),
        "exposure": sum(x["age"] for x in active),
    }

emit.report: active {active} exposure {exposure} | at_risk {at_risk}, compromised {compromised} | created {created} rotated {rotated} revoked {revoked} leaks {leaks}
